name: fossilsense-vscode
description: FossilSense C/C++ best-effort navigation: symbols, outlines, rich hover, ranked definitions, member completion, role-grouped references, and local-only history-aware completion.

viewsWelcome.contents: Place the cursor on a free function, then [Show Call Relations](command:fossilsense.showCallRelations).
Select a relation to inspect its call sites and evidence.

fossilsense.serverPath.description: Path to fossilsense.exe. Empty uses the bundled extension binary, falling back to repository release/debug binaries during extension development.

fossilsense.mode.description: Overall FossilSense server mode. 'auto' starts FossilSense and reports a one-time mutual-exclusion warning if clangd, Microsoft C/C++, or ccls is detected; 'on' starts without that warning; 'off' prevents the server from starting.

fossilsense.completion.mode.description: Controls FossilSense completion, including ordinary identifiers, include paths, and degraded '.'/'->' field/method member completion. 'auto' and 'on' enable completion; 'off' disables completion. C/C++ language-server conflicts are reported by fossilsense.mode, not silently handled per feature.

fossilsense.completionHistory.mode.description: Controls local-only accepted-completion history used as a bounded positive-feedback ranking signal. 'auto' and 'on' record anonymous candidate hashes/buckets and use them for ranking; 'off' disables both. Use 'FossilSense: Clear Completion History' to remove local workspace history.

fossilsense.projectContext.mode.markdownDescription: Controls build-marker project evidence for ordinary identifier completion. `auto` uses the completion file's nearest in-scope marker; `promptOnAmbiguous` also prompts once when an active C/C++ file cannot be assigned while projects are available; `off` enforces baseline completion. Project context never filters candidates or changes navigation, references, coloring, member completion, or include completion.

fossilsense.semanticColoring.mode.description: Controls FossilSense semantic coloring of macros, types, and enum constants. 'auto' and 'on' enable coloring; 'off' disables coloring. C/C++ language-server conflicts are reported by fossilsense.mode, not silently handled per feature. This is a degraded text/index-level colorer and may occasionally mis-color multi-meaning names.

fossilsense.includeScoping.mode.markdownDescription: Limited `#include` reachability scoping. When 'auto' (default), coloring and completion are narrowed to symbols reachable from the current file's resolved include graph: coloring hard-gates to reachable definitions (uncertain files fall back to whole-index behavior), and completion ranks reachable candidates first without dropping any. 'off' reverts both to whole-index behavior. This trades a little recall for far less cross-file noise in large workspaces; it never errors.

fossilsense.references.showRanges.markdownDescription: Show `:line` suffixes in the **Find References (Grouped by Role)** QuickPick. Off by default to keep labels focused on relative paths; selection still navigates to the full returned range.

fossilsense.includePaths.markdownDescription: External C/C++ header reference directories (absolute paths) used for `#include` path completion, `#include` jump-to-header, and degraded symbol indexing of those headers. This is **distinct** from workspace scope `include`/`exclude` (which selects workspace subtrees). Safe to point at a toolchain's include directory, e.g. `C:\TDM-GCC-64\x86_64-w64-mingw32\include`. Missing, inaccessible, or wrong-platform headers are skipped, never errors. Symbols from headers directly `#include`d by a workspace file also participate in semantic coloring; transitively-included ones do not.

fossilsense.debug.candidateReasons.markdownDescription: When enabled, **Go to Definition** logs each candidate's scope tier, confidence, and reason (e.g. `reachable`, `external_first_layer`, `global_fallback`) to the FossilSense output panel, so you can see why candidates ranked as they did. This is a best-effort debug aid; it never changes which definitions are returned. Off by default.

fossilsense.trace.server.description: Controls VS Code language client protocol tracing.

scripts.test: pnpm run compile && node out/test/config.test.js && node out/test/status.test.js && node out/test/serverPath.test.js && node out/test/conflicts.test.js && node out/test/referencesView.test.js && node out/test/callRelations.test.js && node out/test/completionHistory.test.js && node out/test/projectContext.test.js
